NKJV.1: Moreover, brethren, I do not want you to be unaware that all our fathers were under the cloud, all passed through the sea,
NKJV.2: all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea,
NKJV.3: all ate the same spiritual food,
NKJV.4: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them, and that Rock was Christ.
NKJV.5: But with most of them God was not well pleased, for their bodies were scattered in the wilderness.
NKJV.6: Now these things became our examples, to the intent that we should not lust after evil things as they also lusted.
NKJV.7: And do not become idolaters as were some of them. As it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.”
NKJV.8: Nor let us commit sexual immorality, as some of them did, and in one day twenty-three thousand fell;
NKJV.9: nor let us tempt Christ, as some of them also tempted, and were destroyed by serpents;
NKJV.10: nor complain, as some of them also complained, and were destroyed by the destroyer.
NKJV.11: Now all these things happened to them as examples, and they were written for our admonition, upon whom the ends of the ages have come.
NKJV.12: Therefore let him who thinks he stands take heed lest he fall.
NKJV.13: No temptation has overtaken you except such as is common to man; but God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted beyond what you are able, but with the temptation will also make the way of escape, that you may be able to bear it.
NKJV.14: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
NKJV.15: I speak as to wise men; judge for yourselves what I say.
NKJV.16: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
NKJV.17: For we, though many, are one bread and one body; for we all partake of that one bread.
NKJV.18: Observe Israel after the flesh: Are not those who eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
NKJV.19: What am I saying then? That an idol is anything, or what is offered to idols is anything?
NKJV.20: Rather, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice they sacrifice to demons and not to God, and I do not want you to have fellowship with demons.
NKJV.21: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cannot partake of the Lord’s table and of the table of demons.
NKJV.22: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than He?
NKJV.23: All things are lawful for me, but not all things are helpful; all things are lawful for me, but not all things edify.
NKJV.24: Let no one seek his own, but each one the other’s well-being.
NKJV.25: Eat whatever is sold in the meat market, asking no questions for conscience’ sake;
NKJV.26: for “the earth is the LORD’s, and all its fullness.”
NKJV.27: If any of those who do not believe invites you to dinner, and you desire to go, eat whatever is set before you, asking no question for conscience’ sake.
NKJV.28: But if anyone says to you, “This was offered to idols,” do not eat it for the sake of the one who told you, and for conscience’ sake; for “the earth is the LORD’s, and all its fullness.”
NKJV.29: “Conscience,” I say, not your own, but that of the other. For why is my liberty judged by another man’s conscience?
NKJV.30: But if I partake with thanks, why am I evil spoken of for the food over which I give thanks?
NKJV.31: Therefore, whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.
NKJV.32: Give no offense, either to the Jews or to the Greeks or to the church of God,
NKJV.33: just as I also please all men in all things, not seeking my own profit, but the profit of many, that they may be saved.

ESV.1: For I do not want you to be unaware, brothers,  that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea,
ESV.2: and all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea,
ESV.3: and all ate the same spiritual food,
ESV.4: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank from the spiritual Rock that followed them, and the Rock was Christ.
ESV.5: Nevertheless, with most of them God was not pleased, for they were overthrown  in the wilderness.
ESV.6: Now these things took place as examples for us, that we might not desire evil as they did.
ESV.7: Do not be idolaters as some of them were; as it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink and rose up to play.”
ESV.8: We must not indulge in sexual immorality as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand fell in a single day.
ESV.9: We must not put Christ  to the test, as some of them did and were destroyed by serpents,
ESV.10: nor grumble, as some of them did and were destroyed by the Destroyer.
ESV.11: Now these things happened to them as an example, but they were written down for our instruction, on whom the end of the ages has come.
ESV.12: Therefore let anyone who thinks that he stands take heed lest he fall.
ESV.13: No temptation has overtaken you that is not common to man. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tempted beyond your ability, but with the temptation he will also provide the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.
ESV.14: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
ESV.15: I speak as to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
ESV.16: The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not a participation in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not a participation in the body of Christ?
ESV.17: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one bread.
ESV.18: Consider the people of Israel:  are not those who eat the sacrifices participants in the altar?
ESV.19: What do I imply then? That food offered to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
ESV.20: No, I imply that what pagans sacrifice they offer to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be participants with demons.
ESV.21: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
ESV.22: Shall we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
ESV.23: “All things are lawful,” but not all things are helpful. “All things are lawful,” but not all things build up.
ESV.24: Let no one seek his own good, but the good of his neighbor.
ESV.25: Eat whatever is sold in the meat market without raising any question on the ground of conscience.
ESV.26: For “the earth is the Lord's, and the fullness thereof.”
ESV.27: If one of the unbelievers invites you to dinner and you are disposed to go, eat whatever is set before you without raising any question on the ground of conscience.
ESV.28: But if someone says to you, “This has been offered in sacrifice,” then do not eat it, for the sake of the one who informed you, and for the sake of conscience —
ESV.29: I do not mean your conscience, but his. For why should my liberty be determined by someone else's conscience?
ESV.30: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I denounced because of that for which I give thanks?
ESV.31: So, whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.
ESV.32: Give no offense to Jews or to Greeks or to the church of God,
ESV.33: just as I try to please everyone in everything I do, not seeking my own advantage, but that of many, that they may be saved.

NASB.1: For I do not want you to be unaware, brethren, that our fathers were all under the cloud and all passed through the sea;
NASB.2: and all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
NASB.3: and all ate the same spiritual food;
NASB.4: and all drank the same spiritual drink, for they were drinking from a spiritual rock which followed them; and the rock was Christ.
NASB.5: Nevertheless, with most of them God was not well-pleased; for they were laid low in the wilderness.
NASB.6: Now these things happened as examples for us, so that we would not crave evil things as they also craved.
NASB.7: Do not be idolaters, as some of them were; as it is written, 'THE PEOPLE SAT DOWN TO EAT AND DRINK, AND STOOD UP TO PLAY.'
NASB.8: Nor let us act immorally, as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand fell in one day.
NASB.9: Nor let us try the Lord, as some of them did, and were destroyed by the serpents.
NASB.10: Nor grumble, as some of them did, and were destroyed by the destroyer.
NASB.11: Now these things happened to them as an example, and they were written for our instruction, upon whom the ends of the ages have come.
NASB.12: Therefore let him who thinks he stands take heed that he does not fall.
NASB.13: No temptation has overtaken you but such as is common to man; and God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted beyond what you are able, but with the temptation will provide the way of escape also, so that you will be able to endure it.
NASB.14: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
NASB.15: I speak as to wise men; you judge what I say.
NASB.16: Is not the cup of blessing which we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Is not the bread which we break a sharing in the body of Christ?
NASB.17: Since there is one bread, we who are many are one body; for we all partake of the one bread.
NASB.18: Look at the nation Israel; are not those who eat the sacrifices sharers in the altar?
NASB.19: What do I mean then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
NASB.20: [No], but [I say] that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God; and I do not want you to become sharers in demons.
NASB.21: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
NASB.22: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? We are not stronger than He, are we?
NASB.23: All things are lawful, but not all things are profitable. All things are lawful, but not all things edify.
NASB.24: Let no one seek his own [good], but that of his neighbor.
NASB.25: Eat anything that is sold in the meat market without asking questions for conscience' sake;
NASB.26: FOR THE EARTH IS THE LORD'S, AND ALL IT CONTAINS.
NASB.27: If one of the unbelievers invites you and you want to go, eat anything that is set before you without asking questions for conscience' sake.
NASB.28: But if anyone says to you, 'This is meat sacrificed to idols,' do not eat [it], for the sake of the one who informed [you], and for conscience' sake;
NASB.29: I mean not your own conscience, but the other [man's]; for why is my freedom judged by another's conscience?
NASB.30: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I slandered concerning that for which I give thanks?
NASB.31: Whether, then, you eat or drink or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.
NASB.32: Give no offense either to Jews or to Greeks or to the church of God;
NASB.33: just as I also please all men in all things, not seeking my own profit but the [profit] of the many, so that they may be saved.

NLT.1: I don’t want you to forget, dear brothers and sisters, about our ancestors in the wilderness long ago. All of them were guided by a cloud that moved ahead of them, and all of them walked through the sea on dry ground.
NLT.2: In the cloud and in the sea, all of them were baptized as followers of Moses.
NLT.3: All of them ate the same spiritual food,
NLT.4: and all of them drank the same spiritual water. For they drank from the spiritual rock that traveled with them, and that rock was Christ.
NLT.5: Yet God was not pleased with most of them, and their bodies were scattered in the wilderness.
NLT.6: These things happened as a warning to us, so that we would not crave evil things as they did,
NLT.7: or worship idols as some of them did. As the Scriptures say, “The people celebrated with feasting and drinking, and they indulged in pagan revelry.”
NLT.8: And we must not engage in sexual immorality as some of them did, causing 23,000 of them to die in one day.
NLT.9: Nor should we put Christ to the test, as some of them did and then died from snakebites.
NLT.10: And don’t grumble as some of them did, and then were destroyed by the angel of death.
NLT.11: These things happened to them as examples for us. They were written down to warn us who live at the end of the age.
NLT.12: If you think you are standing strong, be careful not to fall.
NLT.13: The temptations in your life are no different from what others experience. And God is faithful. He will not allow the temptation to be more than you can stand. When you are tempted, he will show you a way out so that you can endure.
NLT.14: So, my dear friends, flee from the worship of idols.
NLT.15: You are reasonable people. Decide for yourselves if what I am saying is true.
NLT.16: When we bless the cup at the Lord’s Table, aren’t we sharing in the blood of Christ? And when we break the bread, aren’t we sharing in the body of Christ?
NLT.17: And though we are many, we all eat from one loaf of bread, showing that we are one body.
NLT.18: Think about the people of Israel. Weren’t they united by eating the sacrifices at the altar?
NLT.19: What am I trying to say? Am I saying that food offered to idols has some significance, or that idols are real gods?
NLT.20: No, not at all. I am saying that these sacrifices are offered to demons, not to God. And I don’t want you to participate with demons.
NLT.21: You cannot drink from the cup of the Lord and from the cup of demons, too. You cannot eat at the Lord’s Table and at the table of demons, too.
NLT.22: What? Do we dare to rouse the Lord’s jealousy? Do you think we are stronger than he is?
NLT.23: You say, “I am allowed to do anything” — but not everything is good for you. You say, “I am allowed to do anything” — but not everything is beneficial.
NLT.24: Don’t be concerned for your own good but for the good of others.
NLT.25: So you may eat any meat that is sold in the marketplace without raising questions of conscience.
NLT.26: For “the earth is the LORD’s, and everything in it.”
NLT.27: If someone who isn’t a believer asks you home for dinner, accept the invitation if you want to. Eat whatever is offered to you without raising questions of conscience.
NLT.28: (But suppose someone tells you, “This meat was offered to an idol.” Don’t eat it, out of consideration for the conscience of the one who told you.
NLT.29: It might not be a matter of conscience for you, but it is for the other person.) For why should my freedom be limited by what someone else thinks?
NLT.30: If I can thank God for the food and enjoy it, why should I be condemned for eating it?
NLT.31: So whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do it all for the glory of God.
NLT.32: Don’t give offense to Jews or Gentiles or the church of God.
NLT.33: I, too, try to please everyone in everything I do. I don’t just do what is best for me; I do what is best for others so that many may be saved.

WEB.1: Now I would not have you ignorant, brothers, that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea;
WEB.2: and were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
WEB.3: and all ate the same spiritual food;
WEB.4: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank of a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
WEB.5: However with most of them, God was not well pleased, for they were overthrown in the wilderness.
WEB.6: Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things, as they also lusted.
WEB.7: Don’t be idolaters, as some of them were. As it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.”
WEB.8: Let us not commit sexual immorality, as some of them committed, and in one day twenty-three thousand fell.
WEB.9: Let us not test Christ,  as some of them tested, and perished by the serpents.
WEB.10: Don’t grumble, as some of them also grumbled, and perished by the destroyer.
WEB.11: Now all these things happened to them by way of example, and they were written for our admonition, on whom the ends of the ages have come.
WEB.12: Therefore let him who thinks he stands be careful that he doesn’t fall.
WEB.13: No temptation has taken you except what is common to man. God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted above what you are able, but will with the temptation also make the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.
WEB.14: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
WEB.15: I speak as to wise men. Judge what I say.
WEB.16: The cup of blessing which we bless, isn’t it a sharing of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, isn’t it a sharing of the body of Christ?
WEB.17: Because there is one loaf of bread, we, who are many, are one body; for we all partake of the one loaf of bread.
WEB.18: Consider Israel according to the flesh. Don’t those who eat the sacrifices participate in the altar?
WEB.19: What am I saying then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
WEB.20: But I say that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons, and not to God, and I don’t desire that you would have fellowship with demons.
WEB.21: You can’t both drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You can’t both partake of the table of the Lord, and of the table of demons.
WEB.22: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
WEB.23: “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are profitable. “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things build up.
WEB.24: Let no one seek his own, but each one his neighbor’s good.
WEB.25: Whatever is sold in the butcher shop, eat, asking no question for the sake of conscience,
WEB.26: for “the earth is the Lord’s, and its fullness.”
WEB.27: But if one of those who don’t believe invites you to a meal, and you are inclined to go, eat whatever is set before you, asking no questions for the sake of conscience.
WEB.28: But if anyone says to you, “This was offered to idols,” don’t eat it for the sake of the one who told you, and for the sake of conscience. For “the earth is the Lord’s, and all its fullness.”
WEB.29: Conscience, I say, not your own, but the other’s conscience. For why is my liberty judged by another conscience?
WEB.30: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I denounced for that for which I give thanks?
WEB.31: Whether therefore you eat, or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.
WEB.32: Give no occasion for stumbling, either to Jews, or to Greeks, or to the assembly of God;
WEB.33: even as I also please all men in all things, not seeking my own profit, but the profit of the many, that they may be saved.

CSB17.1: Now I do not want you to be unaware, brothers and sisters, that our ancestors <sup>ⓝ</sup> were all under the cloud, <sup>ⓐ</sup> all passed through the sea, <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.2: and all were baptized <sup>ⓒ</sup> into Moses <sup>ⓓ</sup> in the cloud and in the sea.
CSB17.3: They all ate the same spiritual food, <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.4: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank from the spiritual rock <sup>ⓕ</sup> that followed them, and that rock was Christ. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.5: Nevertheless God was not pleased with most of them, since they were struck down in the wilderness. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.6: Now these things took place as examples for us, so that we will not desire <sup>ⓘ</sup> evil things as they did. <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.7: Don’t become idolaters ⓚ as some of them were; as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink, and got up to party. [2] [3] ⓛ
CSB17.8: Let us not commit sexual immorality <sup>ⓜ</sup> as some of them did, <sup>[4]</sup> and in a single day twenty-three thousand people died. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.9: Let us not test Christ as some of them did <sup>[5]</sup> and were destroyed by snakes. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.10: And don’t complain <sup>ⓑ</sup> as some of them did, <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup> and were killed by the destroyer. <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.11: These things happened to them as examples, and they were written for our instruction, <sup>ⓔ</sup> on whom the ends of the ages <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup> have come. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.12: So, whoever thinks he stands must be careful not to fall. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.13: No temptation has come upon you except what is common to humanity. But God is faithful; <sup>ⓘ</sup> he will not allow you to be tempted beyond what you are able, but with the temptation he will also provide a way out <sup>ⓙ</sup> so that you may be able to bear it.
CSB17.14: So then, my dear friends, flee from idolatry.
CSB17.15: I am speaking as to sensible people. Judge for yourselves what I am saying.
CSB17.16: The cup <sup>ⓚ</sup> of blessing <sup>ⓛ</sup> that we bless, is it not a sharing in the blood of Christ? The bread <sup>ⓜ</sup> that we break, is it not a sharing in the body of Christ?
CSB17.17: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one <sup>ⓝ</sup> body, since all of us share the one bread.
CSB17.18: Consider the people of Israel. <sup>[9]</sup> Do not those who eat the sacrifices participate in the altar? <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.19: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
CSB17.20: No, but I do say that what they <sup>[10]</sup> sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be participants with demons!
CSB17.21: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot share in the Lord’s table and the table of demons.
CSB17.22: Or are we provoking the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he? <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.23: “Everything is permissible,” <sup>[11]</sup> but not everything is beneficial. “Everything is permissible,” <sup>[12]</sup> but not everything builds up.
CSB17.24: No one is to seek his own good, but the good of the other person. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.25: Eat everything that is sold in the meat market, without raising questions for the sake of conscience,
CSB17.26: since the earth is the Lord’s, ⓓ and all that is in it. [13] ⓔ
CSB17.27: If any of the unbelievers invites you over and you want to go, eat everything that is set before you, without raising questions for the sake of conscience.
CSB17.28: But if someone says to you, “This is food from a sacrifice,” do not eat it, <sup>ⓕ</sup> out of consideration for the one who told you, and for the sake of conscience. <sup>[14]</sup>
CSB17.29: I do not mean your own conscience, but the other person’s. For why is my freedom judged <sup>ⓖ</sup> by another person’s conscience?
CSB17.30: If I partake with thanksgiving, <sup>ⓗ</sup> why am I criticized because of something for which I give thanks?
CSB17.31: So, whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do everything for the glory of God. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.32: Give no offense <sup>ⓙ</sup> to Jews or Greeks or the church of God,
CSB17.33: just as I also try to please everyone in everything, not seeking my own benefit, but the benefit of many, <sup>ⓚ</sup> so that they may be saved.

AMP.1: For I do not want you to be unaware, believers, that our fathers were all under the cloud [in which God's presence went before them] and they all passed [miraculously and safely] through the [Red] Sea;
AMP.2: And all [of them] were baptized into Moses [into his safekeeping as their leader] in the cloud and in the sea;
AMP.3: and all [of them] ate the same spiritual food;
AMP.4: and all [of them] drank the same spiritual drink, for they were drinking from a spiritual rock which followed them; and the rock was Christ.
AMP.5: Nevertheless, God was not well-pleased with most of them, for they were scattered along the ground in the wilderness [because their lack of self-control led to disobedience which led to death].
AMP.6: Now these things [the warnings and admonitions] took place as examples for us, so that we would not crave evil things as they did.
AMP.7: Do not be worshipers of handmade gods, as some of them were; just as it is written [in Scripture], "The people sat down to eat and drink [after sacrificing to the golden calf at Horeb], and stood up to play [indulging in immoral activities]."
AMP.8: We must not indulge in [nor tolerate] sexual immorality, as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand [suddenly] fell [dead] in a single day!
AMP.9: We must not tempt the Lord [that is, test His patience, question His purpose or exploit His goodness], as some of them did—and they were killed by serpents.
AMP.10: And do not murmur [in unwarranted discontent], as some of them did—and were destroyed by the destroyer.
AMP.11: Now these things happened to them as an example and warning [to us]; they were written for our instruction [to admonish and equip us], upon whom the ends of the ages have come.
AMP.12: Therefore let the one who thinks he stands firm [immune to temptation, being overconfident and self-righteous], take care that he does not fall [into sin and condemnation].
AMP.13: No temptation [regardless of its source] has overtaken or enticed you that is not common to human experience [nor is any temptation unusual or beyond human resistance]; but God is faithful [to His word—He is compassionate and trustworthy], and He will not let you be tempted beyond your ability [to resist], but along with the temptation He [has in the past and is now and] will [always] provide the way out as well, so that you will be able to endure it [without yielding, and will overcome temptation with joy].
AMP.14: Therefore, my beloved, run [keep far, far away] from [any sort of] idolatry [and that includes loving anything more than God, or participating in anything that leads to sin and enslaves the soul].
AMP.15: I am speaking as to wise and sensible people; judge [carefully and thoughtfully consider] for yourselves what I say.
AMP.16: Is the cup of blessing which we bless [at the Lord's Supper] not a sharing in the blood of Christ? [Indeed it is.] Is the bread which we break not a sharing in the body of Christ? [Indeed it is.]
AMP.17: Since there is one bread, we [believers] who are many are [united into] one body; for we all partake of the one bread [which represents the body of Christ].
AMP.18: Consider the people of Israel; are those who eat the sacrifices not partners of the altar [united in their worship of the same God]? [Indeed they are.]
AMP.19: What do I mean then? That a thing offered to idols is anything [special or changed simply because it is offered], or that an idol is anything?
AMP.20: On the contrary, the things which the Gentiles (pagans) sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons [in effect], and not to God; and I do not want you to become partners with demons [by eating at feasts in pagan temples].
AMP.21: You cannot drink [both] the Lord's cup and the cup of demons. You cannot share in both the Lord's table and the table of demons [thereby becoming partners with them].
AMP.22: Do we [really] provoke the Lord to jealousy [when we eat food sacrificed to handmade "gods" at pagan feasts]? Are we [spiritually] stronger than He? [Certainly not! He knows that the idols are nothing. But we deeply offend Him.]
AMP.23: All things are lawful [that is, morally legitimate, permissible], but not all things are beneficial or advantageous. All things are lawful, but not all things are constructive [to character] and edifying [to spiritual life].
AMP.24: Let no one seek [only] his own good, but [also] that of the other person.
AMP.25: [Regarding meat offered to idols:] Eat anything that is sold in the meat market without asking any questions for the sake of your conscience,
AMP.26: For the [whole] earth is the Lord's, and everything that is in it.
AMP.27: If one of the unbelievers invites you [to a meal at his home] and you want to go, eat whatever is served to you without asking questions [about its source] for the sake of your conscience.
AMP.28: But if anyone says to you, "This meat has been offered in sacrifice to an idol," do not eat it, out of consideration for the one who told you, and for conscience's sake—
AMP.29: and by conscience I mean for the sake of the other man's, not yours. For why is my freedom [of choice] judged by another's conscience [another's ethics—another's sense of right and wrong]?
AMP.30: If I take my share [of food] with thankfulness, why am I accused because of something for which I give thanks?
AMP.31: So then, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, do all to the glory of [our great] God.
AMP.32: Do not offend Jews or Greeks or even the church of God [but live to honor Him];
AMP.33: just as I please everyone in all things [as much as possible adapting myself to the interests of others], not seeking my own benefit but that of the many, so that they [will be open to the message of salvation and] may be saved.

MSG.1: Remember our history, friends, and be warned. All our ancestors were led by the providential Cloud and taken miraculously through the Sea.
MSG.2: They went through the waters, in a baptism like ours, as Moses led them from enslaving death to salvation life.
MSG.4: and drank identical food and drink, meals provided daily by God. They drank from the Rock, God's fountain for them that stayed with them wherever they were. And the Rock was Christ.
MSG.5: But just experiencing God's wonder and grace didn't seem to mean much—most of them were defeated by temptation during the hard times in the desert, and God was not pleased.
MSG.6: The same thing could happen to us. We must be on guard so that we never get caught up in wanting our own way as they did.
MSG.7: And we must not turn our religion into a circus as they did—"First the people partied, then they threw a dance."
MSG.8: We must not be sexually promiscuous—they paid for that, remember, with twenty-three thousand deaths in one day!
MSG.9: We must never try to get Christ to serve us instead of us serving him; they tried it, and God launched an epidemic of poisonous snakes.
MSG.10: We must be careful not to stir up discontent; discontent destroyed them.
MSG.11: These are all warning markers—danger!—in our history books, written down so that we don't repeat their mistakes. Our positions in the story are parallel—they at the beginning, we at the end—and we are just as capable of messing it up as they were.
MSG.12: Don't be so naive and self-confident. You're not exempt. You could fall flat on your face as easily as anyone else. Forget about self-confidence; it's useless. Cultivate God-confidence.
MSG.13: No test or temptation that comes your way is beyond the course of what others have had to face. All you need to remember is that God will never let you down; he'll never let you be pushed past your limit; he'll always be there to help you come through it.
MSG.14: So, my very dear friends, when you see people reducing God to something they can use or control, get out of their company as fast as you can.
MSG.15: I assume I'm addressing believers now who are mature. Draw your own conclusions:
MSG.16: When we drink the cup of blessing, aren't we taking into ourselves the blood, the very life, of Christ? And isn't it the same with the loaf of bread we break and eat? Don't we take into ourselves the body, the very life, of Christ?
MSG.17: Because there is one loaf, our many-ness becomes one-ness—Christ doesn't become fragmented in us. Rather, we become unified in him. We don't reduce Christ to what we are; he raises us to what he is.
MSG.18: That's basically what happened even in old Israel—those who ate the sacrifices offered on God's altar entered into God's action at the altar.
MSG.19: Do you see the difference? Sacrifices offered to idols are offered to nothing, for what's the idol but a nothing?
MSG.20: Or worse than nothing, a minus, a demon! I don't want you to become part of something that reduces you to less than yourself.
MSG.21: And you can't have it both ways, banqueting with the Master one day and slumming with demons the next.
MSG.22: Besides, the Master won't put up with it. He wants us—all or nothing. Do you think you can get off with anything less?
MSG.23: Looking at it one way, you could say, "Anything goes. Because of God's immense generosity and grace, we don't have to dissect and scrutinize every action to see if it will pass muster." But the point is not to just get by.
MSG.24: We want to live well, but our foremost efforts should be to help others live well.
MSG.25: With that as a base to work from, common sense can take you the rest of the way. Eat anything sold at the butcher shop, for instance; you don't have to run an "idolatry test" on every item.
MSG.26: "The earth," after all, "is God's, and everything in it." That "everything" certainly includes the leg of lamb in the butcher shop.
MSG.27: If a nonbeliever invites you to dinner and you feel like going, go ahead and enjoy yourself; eat everything placed before you. It would be both bad manners and bad spirituality to cross-examine your host on the ethical purity of each course as it is served.
MSG.28: On the other hand, if he goes out of his way to tell you that this or that was sacrificed to god or goddess so-and-so, you should pass. Even though you may be indifferent as to where it came from, he isn't, and you don't want to send mixed messages to him about who you are worshiping.
MSG.29: But, except for these special cases, I'm not going to walk around on eggshells worrying about what small-minded people might say; I'm going to stride free and easy, knowing what our large-minded Master has already said.
MSG.30: If I eat what is served to me, grateful to God for what is on the table, how can I worry about what someone will say? I thanked God for it and he blessed it!
MSG.31: So eat your meals heartily, not worrying about what others say about you—you're eating to God's glory, after all, not to please them. As a matter of fact, do everything that way, heartily and freely to God's glory.
MSG.32: At the same time, don't be callous in your exercise of freedom, thoughtlessly stepping on the toes of those who aren't as free as you are.
MSG.33: I try my best to be considerate of everyone's feelings in all these matters; I hope you will be, too.

NIV.1:
Warnings From Israel’s History
For I do not want you to be ignorant of the fact, brothers, that our forefathers were all under the cloud and that they all passed through the sea.
NIV.2: They were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
NIV.3: They all ate the same spiritual food
NIV.4: and drank the same spiritual drink; for they drank from the spiritual rock that accompanied them, and that rock was Christ.
NIV.5: Nevertheless, God was not pleased with most of them; their bodies were scattered over the desert.
NIV.6: Now these things occurred as examples to keep us from setting our hearts on evil things as they did.
NIV.7: Do not be idolaters, as some of them were; as it is written: “The people sat down to eat and drink and got up to indulge in pagan revelry.”
NIV.8: We should not commit sexual immorality, as some of them did—and in one day twenty-three thousand of them died.
NIV.9: We should not test the Lord, as some of them did—and were killed by snakes.
NIV.10: And do not grumble, as some of them did—and were killed by the destroying angel.
NIV.11: These things happened to them as examples and were written down as warnings for us, on whom the fulfillment of the ages has come.
NIV.12: So, if you think you are standing firm, be careful that you don’t fall!
NIV.13: No temptation has seized you except what is common to man. And God is faithful; he will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear. But when you are tempted, he will also provide a way out so that you can stand up under it.
NIV.14:
Idol Feasts and the Lord’s Supper
Therefore, my dear friends, flee from idolatry.
NIV.15: I speak to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
NIV.16: Is not the cup of thanksgiving for which we give thanks a participation in the blood of Christ? And is not the bread that we break a participation in the body of Christ?
NIV.17: Because there is one loaf, we, who are many, are one body, for we all partake of the one loaf.
NIV.18: Consider the people of Israel: Do not those who eat the sacrifices participate in the altar?
NIV.19: Do I mean then that a sacrifice offered to an idol is anything, or that an idol is anything?
NIV.20: No, but the sacrifices of pagans are offered to demons, not to God, and I do not want you to be participants with demons.
NIV.21: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons too; you cannot have a part in both the Lord’s table and the table of demons.
NIV.22: Are we trying to arouse the Lord’s jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
NIV.23:
The Believer’s Freedom
“Everything is permissible”—but not everything is beneficial. “Everything is permissible”—but not everything is constructive.
NIV.24: Nobody should seek his own good, but the good of others.
NIV.25: Eat anything sold in the meat market without raising questions of conscience,
NIV.26: for, “The earth is the Lord’s, and everything in it.”
NIV.27: If some unbeliever invites you to a meal and you want to go, eat whatever is put before you without raising questions of conscience.
NIV.28: But if anyone says to you, “This has been offered in sacrifice,” then do not eat it, both for the sake of the man who told you and for conscience’ sake—
NIV.29: the other man’s conscience, I mean, not yours. For why should my freedom be judged by another’s conscience?
NIV.30: If I take part in the meal with thankfulness, why am I denounced because of something I thank God for?
NIV.31: So whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, do it all for the glory of God.
NIV.32: Do not cause anyone to stumble, whether Jews, Greeks or the church of God—
NIV.33: even as I try to please everybody in every way. For I am not seeking my own good but the good of many, so that they may be saved.

NRSVCE.1: I do not want you to be unaware, brothers and sisters, that our ancestors were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea,
NRSVCE.2: and all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea,
NRSVCE.3: and all ate the same spiritual food,
NRSVCE.4: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank from the spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
NRSVCE.5: Nevertheless, God was not pleased with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
NRSVCE.6: Now these things occurred as examples for us, so that we might not desire evil as they did.
NRSVCE.7: Do not become idolaters as some of them did; as it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and they rose up to play.”
NRSVCE.8: We must not indulge in sexual immorality as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand fell in a single day.
NRSVCE.9: We must not put Christ to the test, as some of them did, and were destroyed by serpents.
NRSVCE.10: And do not complain as some of them did, and were destroyed by the destroyer.
NRSVCE.11: These things happened to them to serve as an example, and they were written down to instruct us, on whom the ends of the ages have come.
NRSVCE.12: So if you think you are standing, watch out that you do not fall.
NRSVCE.13: No testing has overtaken you that is not common to everyone. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tested beyond your strength, but with the testing he will also provide the way out so that you may be able to endure it.
NRSVCE.14: Therefore, my dear friends, flee from the worship of idols.
NRSVCE.15: I speak as to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
NRSVCE.16: The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not a sharing in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not a sharing in the body of Christ?
NRSVCE.17: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one bread.
NRSVCE.18: Consider the people of Israel; are not those who eat the sacrifices partners in the altar?
NRSVCE.19: What do I imply then? That food sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
NRSVCE.20: No, I imply that what pagans sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be partners with demons.
NRSVCE.21: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
NRSVCE.22: Or are we provoking the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
NRSVCE.23: “All things are lawful,” but not all things are beneficial. “All things are lawful,” but not all things build up.
NRSVCE.24: Do not seek your own advantage, but that of the other.
NRSVCE.25: Eat whatever is sold in the meat market without raising any question on the ground of conscience,
NRSVCE.26: for “the earth and its fullness are the Lord’s.”
NRSVCE.27: If an unbeliever invites you to a meal and you are disposed to go, eat whatever is set before you without raising any question on the ground of conscience.
NRSVCE.28: But if someone says to you, “This has been offered in sacrifice,” then do not eat it, out of consideration for the one who informed you, and for the sake of conscience —
NRSVCE.29: I mean the other’s conscience, not your own. For why should my liberty be subject to the judgment of someone else’s conscience?
NRSVCE.30: If I partake with thankfulness, why should I be denounced because of that for which I give thanks?
NRSVCE.31: So, whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do everything for the glory of God.
NRSVCE.32: Give no offense to Jews or to Greeks or to the church of God,
NRSVCE.33: just as I try to please everyone in everything I do, not seeking my own advantage, but that of many, so that they may be saved.

NET.1: For I do not want you to be unaware, brothers and sisters, that our fathers were all under the cloud and all passed through the sea,
NET.2: and all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea,
NET.3: and all ate the same spiritual food,
NET.4: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they were all drinking from the spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
NET.5: But God was not pleased with most of them, for they were cut down in the wilderness.
NET.6: These things happened as examples for us, so that we will not crave evil things as they did.
NET.7: So do not be idolaters, as some of them were. As it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink and rose up to play.”
NET.8: And let us not be immoral, as some of them were, and twenty-three thousand died in a single day.
NET.9: And let us not put Christ to the test, as some of them did, and were destroyed by snakes.
NET.10: And do not complain, as some of them did, and were killed by the destroying angel.
NET.11: These things happened to them as examples and were written for our instruction, on whom the ends of the ages have come.
NET.12: So let the one who thinks he is standing be careful that he does not fall.
NET.13: No trial has overtaken you that is not faced by others. And God is faithful: He will not let you be tried beyond what you are able to bear, but with the trial will also provide a way out so that you may be able to endure it.
NET.14: So then, my dear friends, flee from idolatry.
NET.15: I am speaking to thoughtful people. Consider what I say.
NET.16: Is not the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Is not the bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?
NET.17: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all share the one bread.
NET.18: Look at the people of Israel. Are not those who eat the sacrifices partners in the altar?
NET.19: Am I saying that idols or food sacrificed to them amount to anything?
NET.20: No, I mean that what the pagans sacrifice is to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be partners with demons.
NET.21: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot take part in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
NET.22: Or are we trying to provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we really stronger than he is?
NET.23: “Everything is lawful,” but not everything is beneficial. “Everything is lawful,” but not everything builds others up.
NET.24: Do not seek your own good, but the good of the other person.
NET.25: Eat anything that is sold in the marketplace without questions of conscience,
NET.26: for the earth and its abundance are the Lord’s.
NET.27: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner and you want to go, eat whatever is served without asking questions of conscience.
NET.28: But if someone says to you, “This is from a sacrifice,” do not eat, because of the one who told you and because of conscience —
NET.29: I do not mean yours but the other person’s. For why is my freedom being judged by another’s conscience?
NET.30: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I blamed for the food that I give thanks for?
NET.31: So whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do everything for the glory of God.
NET.32: Do not give offense to Jews or Greeks or to the church of God,
NET.33: just as I also try to please everyone in all things. I do not seek my own benefit, but the benefit of many, so that they may be saved.
